description_long: This object is a compact solar energy infrastructure unit, featuring a rectangular solar panel mounted on a robust frame. The panel is dark, likely made of photovoltaic cells, and is supported by a metallic structure with visible brackets and fasteners. The base appears to house electrical components, possibly including an inverter or control box, with some colored markings and labels. The overall design is utilitarian, with a focus on durability and efficient energy capture. The structure is relatively low-profile, suggesting it is intended for ground or low-mount urban installations. The visible surfaces are primarily metallic and glassy, with some plastic or composite elements for insulation and protection.
description: A compact, rectangular solar panel mounted on a sturdy metallic frame with a base housing electrical components. Designed for efficient solar energy capture in urban environments.
description_view_0: Front view showing the face of the solar panel and the main housing with visible labels and colored markings.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the thin profile of the solar panel and the supporting frame structure.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the back of the panel and the base, with less detail on the electrical housing.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the angled panel and the mounting structure, with some colored elements at the base.
attribute_panel_orientation: tilted at approximately 20 degrees, azimuth direction not clearly discernible, orientation appears fixed
attribute_mounting_structure_type: ground-mounted with an aluminum frame and steel supports
